surface_verification.runner: uv run --with pytest python -m pytest integration-tests/test_bloat_gate_fanout.py shared/tests/test_bloat_gate_fanout_dedup.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
surface_verification.justification: Behavior is a Claude Code Stop hook (Python CLI). The fan-out integration test drives the REAL hook script across all 12 plugin roots in a git-backed project; cli surface, tests_run=6. No web/API/UI/store/SSE surface — the bloat gate is a local dev-time hook, not consumed by any client.

behaviors.behavior: N fan-out invocations with offenders (same Stop, same session) -> exactly one decision:block, the rest empty stdout (register-everywhere hook + claim guard COMPOSE under a real 12-plugin fan-out)
behaviors.evidence: integration test_bloat_gate_fanout.py::test_bloat_gate_fanout_blocks_once + ::test_bloat_gate_fanout_concurrent_one_blocker (drives the real hook across all 12 plugin roots in a git project, sequential + parallel); unit test_bloat_gate_fanout_dedup.py::test_fanout_dedup_blocks_once_then_passes PASSED

behaviors.behavior: claim is per (event, session): a second session still blocks once (no cross-session suppression)
single firing with offenders still blocks (no regression to existing gate behavior)
pass-path invocation (no offender) never creates/consumes the claim file (claim-after-no-op-guards ordering)
sid == 'unknown' -> helper does the work without claiming (fail-open over-emit, never a dropped block)
behaviors.evidence: existing test_bloat_gate_on_stop.py::test_session_scoping_unknown_fallback PASSED (still blocks under unknown sid)

behaviors.behavior: a genuinely-later Stop (>30s) re-arms and re-blocks a still-oversize file
behaviors.evidence: no new TTL logic in this diff; the 30s-TTL re-arm is owned and proven by shared/tests/test_event_once.py
